rationale.completeness: Fully addresses all requirements: adds --output-format CLI flag, implements JSON/CSV/markdown formats, and includes summary statistics in all three formats.
rationale.correctness: The CSV format uses comment lines starting with '#' which are non-standard and will break most CSV parsers; also, values containing commas (e.g. question text, categories) are only partially escaped since the manual string formatting doesn't handle all edge cases that Python's csv module would handle correctly.
rationale.style: Follows project conventions reasonably well—type hints, docstrings, argparse patterns match existing code; minor issue is lazy imports of 'io' inside functions rather than at module level with other imports.
rationale.quality: The three format functions are cleanly separated with good abstraction via save_results(), but generate_summary_stats() duplicates data already present in the results dict, and the CSV formatter manually builds output line-by-line instead of using Python's built-in csv module, making it fragile and harder to maintain.
